headline: Citizen Scientists Unlock Mystery of Parental Care Evolution
meta_description: Citizen scientists used online observations to help researchers solve a long-standing question about how parental care evolved in harvestmen.
summary_short: Working with citizen scientists, researchers discovered how parental care evolved in harvestmen—and completed the project in just days.
body:
An international team of researchers has solved a long-standing biological mystery with help from thousands of citizen scientists. By analyzing photos and observations contributed through iNaturalist—a platform where the public shares wildlife sightings—they uncovered how parental care behavior first evolved in harvestmen, small arachnids related to spiders.

The citizen science approach proved remarkably powerful. Volunteers more than doubled the number of known cases of egg-guarding behavior in these creatures, providing researchers with a much larger dataset than they could have gathered alone. This expanded evidence revealed something surprising: maternal and paternal care evolved along different paths, each shaped by distinct biological pressures.

The project was completed in just days, demonstrating how modern technology and public participation can accelerate scientific discovery. This success story shows that groundbreaking research doesn't always require expensive laboratories or years of fieldwork. When scientists invite the public to contribute observations, remarkable insights can emerge surprisingly quickly—insights that deepen our understanding of how life on Earth evolved.
